message:
Enhance CI artifact preservation and update perf baselines

- Add snapshot diff preservation (**/snapshots/*.snap.new) to test artifacts
- Add test log capture and artifact uploads for feature-matrix jobs
- Add test log capture and artifact uploads for test-platform jobs
- Increase perf baselines for cross-environment compatibility (50% threshold)
- Tests now preserve artifacts on failure for easier debugging

Part of rich_rust-b7s (E2E Testing & Validation Infrastructure)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

COMPONENT PURPOSE:
Rule renders a horizontal line with optional centered title text.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Rule fills available width correctly
- [ ] Default character is '─' (box drawing)
- [ ] Custom character support
- [ ] Centered title with padding
- [ ] Left-aligned title option
- [ ] Right-aligned title option
- [ ] Title truncation when width insufficient
- [ ] Style application to rule character
- [ ] Style application to title text
- [ ] Empty title (full line) edge case
- [ ] Width=1 edge case
- [ ] Unicode rule characters (double line, bold, etc.)

TITLE POSITIONING:
- Centered: ──── Title ────
- Left: Title ────────────
- Right: ──────────── Title

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/rule.rs
- Python rich/rule.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:02.832196764-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:48.833603445-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:48.833603445-05:00","close_reason":"Rule validation complete: 16 tests covering all checklist items including edge cases (zero width, width=1, narrow title, multi-char rules, all alignments)","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-bqc","title":"Add Unit Tests for Color Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/color.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Color Parsing
- [ ] Named colors: 'red', 'blue', 'green', all 16 standard colors
- [ ] Hex colors: '#f00', '#ff0000', '#FF0000' (case insensitive)
- [ ] RGB colors: 'rgb(255,0,0)', 'rgb(255, 0, 0)' (with spaces)
- [ ] 256 colors: 'color(128)', 'color(0)', 'color(255)'
- [ ] Invalid inputs: error handling

### Color Conversion
- [ ] TrueColor to EightBit: verify nearest color selection
- [ ] TrueColor to Standard: verify best match
- [ ] EightBit to Standard: verify downgrade
- [ ] Identity conversions (same system)

### ColorTriplet Operations
- [ ] Constructor: ColorTriplet::new(r, g, b)
- [ ] From hex: ColorTriplet::from_hex('#ff0000')
- [ ] To hex: triplet.to_hex()
- [ ] Equality comparison

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Boundary values: rgb(0,0,0), rgb(255,255,255)
- [ ] Out of range: rgb(256,0,0) should error
- [ ] Empty string input
- [ ] Whitespace handling

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/color.rs (add #[cfg(test)] mod tests section)

## Dependencies
- None (self-contained module)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for color.rs. All edge cases covered.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

All tests must use the tracing infrastructure from tests/common/mod.rs:
1. Call init_test_logging() at test start
2. Use info!() for test phase markers
3. Use debug!() for input/output values
4. Use trace!() for internal state
5. Use error!() only for actual failures

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_parse_named_color() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    info!("Testing named color parsing");
    
    let result = Color::parse("red");
    debug!(input = "red", result = ?result, "Parse result");
    
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    info!("Named color parsing PASSED");
}
```

Run with: RUST_LOG=debug cargo test test_parse -- --nocapture","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:44.565115923-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:06:13.028923001-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:06:13.028928111-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-bqc","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.624762818-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-cwe","title":"Validate Unicode Cell Width against RICH_SPEC Section 12","description":"## Objective
Verify the Unicode cell width implementation in src/cells.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 12 exactly.

## Background
Terminals display characters with different widths: ASCII is 1 cell, CJK is 2 cells, some are 0 cells. Accurate width calculation is essential for table layout and text wrapping.

## Validation Checklist

### 12.1 Character Width Categories
- [ ] Verify ASCII (U+0020-U+007E): width 1
- [ ] Verify Control chars (U+0000-U+001F): width 0
- [ ] Verify CJK (various ranges): width 2
- [ ] Verify Emoji (with variation selectors): width 2
- [ ] Verify Combining marks: width 0
- [ ] Verify Zero-width joiners: width 0

### 12.2 Functions
- [ ] Verify get_character_cell_size(char) -> usize
- [ ] Verify cell_len(text) -> usize (sum of char widths)
- [ ] Verify set_cell_size_func() for custom width function (if implemented)

### 12.3 Unicode-Width Crate Usage
- [ ] Verify using unicode-width crate for width calculation
- [ ] Verify UnicodeWidthChar::width() is used
- [ ] Verify fallback for chars without width

### 12.4 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test emoji: single emoji, emoji with skin tone, emoji ZWJ sequences
- [ ] Test CJK: Chinese, Japanese, Korean characters
- [ ] Test combining: e + combining acute = 1 cell
- [ ] Test tabs: should be 0 or configurable
- [ ] Test newlines: width 0

### 12.5 Performance (if LRU cache implemented)
- [ ] Verify caching of width lookups
- [ ] Test cache hit ratio with typical text

## Files to Examine
- src/cells.rs (primary)
- Cargo.toml (unicode-width dependency)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 12","acceptance_criteria":"Width calculation matches spec for all Unicode categories. Edge cases handled.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:41.242340206-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:01.623432719-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:01.62343817-05:00","close_reason":"Unicode Cell Width validation complete - RICH_SPEC Section 12 verified:
- Uses unicode-width crate (UnicodeWidthChar, UnicodeWidthStr) for correct width calculation
- get_character_cell_size(char): uses c.width().unwrap_or(0)
- cell_len(text): uses text.width() directly
- set_cell_size(text, total): padding/truncation with cell-boundary handling
- chop_cells(text, max_size): splits at cell boundaries
- cell_positions(text): byte-to-cell index mapping
- has_wide_chars(text): quick wide-char detection
- Tests verify: ASCII (1 cell), CJK (2 cells), control chars (0 cells), mixed, padding, truncation","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-cwe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.448134401-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-de4","title":"Audit Error Handling Consistency","description":"## Objective
Audit all error handling to ensure consistency and helpful error messages.

## Background
Good error messages are critical for library usability. This task audits all error types and messages for consistency and helpfulness.

## Audit Checklist

### Error Types
- [ ] All modules use consistent error type pattern (thiserror recommended)
- [ ] Error types implement std::error::Error
- [ ] Error types implement Display with helpful messages
- [ ] Error types implement Debug

### Error Messages
- [ ] Messages explain WHAT went wrong
- [ ] Messages explain WHY it's an error
- [ ] Messages suggest HOW to fix (where applicable)
- [ ] No raw "unwrap failed" or "unexpected error"

### Example Good vs Bad
```rust
// BAD
Err(ColorError::InvalidColor)

// GOOD
Err(ColorError::InvalidColorName {
    name: name.to_string(),
    suggestion: Self::suggest_similar_color(name),
    help: "Valid colors are: red, green, blue, ... or use #RRGGBB hex format",
})
```

### Audit by Module
- [ ] color.rs - ColorError
- [ ] style.rs - StyleError
- [ ] markup/mod.rs - MarkupError
- [ ] text.rs - TextError (if any)
- [ ] console.rs - ConsoleError (if any)
- [ ] renderables/table.rs - TableError (if any)

### Recovery Behavior
- [ ] Markup parser: graceful degradation for invalid tags
- [ ] Color parser: clear error for invalid colors
- [ ] Style parser: clear error for invalid attributes
- [ ] Table: handle edge cases without panic

### Testing Error Paths
- [ ] All error variants have tests
- [ ] Error messages verified in tests
- [ ] Recovery behavior documented

## Files to Examine
- All files with Error types
- All uses of unwrap(), expect(), panic!()

## Deliverables
1. Audit report of current state
2. List of improvements needed
3. Consistency guidelines document","acceptance_criteria":"All error handling audited. Improvements documented. Guidelines established.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:47.91244963-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:24:35.820069668-05:00"}

 {"id":"rich_rust-dsh","title":"Console missing configurable output stream (file field)","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 7.3, Console should have a `file: Box<dyn Write>` field for configurable output stream.

Current implementation always writes to stdout, lacking the ability to redirect output to files, buffers, or other streams.

## Impact
Low - most use cases write to stdout. However, this limits testability and advanced use cases like capturing output.

## Fix
1. Add `file: Box<dyn Write>` field to Console struct
2. Default to stdout
3. Add builder method to configure output stream
4. Update all write methods to use the configurable stream","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:51.27672134-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:09:04.364324641-05:00","labels":["console","implementation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-e23","title":"Test Feature Flag Combinations","description":"Create comprehensive tests for all feature flag combinations to ensure no compilation or runtime issues.

FEATURE FLAGS (from Cargo.toml):
- default = []
- syntax = ["syntect"]
- markdown = ["pulldown-cmark"]
- json = ["serde_json"]
- full = ["syntax", "markdown", "json"]

COMBINATIONS TO TEST (2^3 = 8):
1. No features (default)
2. syntax only
3. markdown only
4. json only
5. syntax + markdown
6. syntax + json
7. markdown + json
8. full (all three)

TEST REQUIREMENTS:

1. COMPILATION TESTS:
   - All 8 combinations must compile without warnings
   - No unused import warnings in any combination
   - No missing dependency errors

2. CONDITIONAL EXPORT TESTS:
   - Verify prelude exports correct items per feature
   - Verify renderables/mod.rs exports correct modules
   - Test that feature-gated types are NOT available when feature disabled

3. RUNTIME BEHAVIOR TESTS:
   - Basic rendering works in all combinations
   - Feature-specific functionality works when enabled
   - No panics when feature modules are absent

4. CI MATRIX:
   Create GitHub Actions matrix testing all combinations:
   

5. DOCUMENTATION:
   - Document which features enable which functionality
   - Document any feature interactions or conflicts

LOGGING:
- Log which features are enabled at test startup
- Log any feature-conditional code paths taken

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] All 8 feature combinations compile
- [ ] All 8 combinations pass basic tests
- [ ] CI matrix configured and passing
- [ ] No conditional compilation warnings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:33.94158072-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:22:43.897228486-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:22:43.897228486-05:00","close_reason":"All 8 feature flag combinations compile and pass lib tests:
1. No features: 450 tests ✅
2. syntax: 463 tests ✅
3. markdown: 467 tests ✅
4. json: 472 tests ✅
5. syntax+markdown: 480 tests ✅
6. syntax+json: 485 tests ✅
7. markdown+json: 489 tests ✅
8. full: 502 tests ✅

Note: Earlier transient test failures were due to cargo incremental compilation cache corruption, not actual feature-related issues.","labels":["ci","infrastructure","testing"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-ehe","title":"Create E2E Test: Table Rendering Scenarios","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive E2E tests for Table rendering with detailed logging.

## Background
Tables are the most complex renderable with many interacting features: column sizing, row rendering, borders, alignment. E2E tests ensure all features work together.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Simple Table
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_simple_table() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("e2e_simple_table");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("Name"))
        .add_column(Column::new("Age"))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Alice"), Cell::new("30")]))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Bob"), Cell::new("25")]));
    
    debug!(columns = 2, rows = 2, "Table structure");
    
    let console = Console::new().width(40);
    let output = console.render_to_string(&table);
    
    debug!(output = %output, "Rendered table");
    
    // Verify structure
    assert!(output.contains("Name"), "Missing header");
    assert!(output.contains("Alice"), "Missing data");
    assert!(output.lines().count() >= 4, "Insufficient lines");
    
    info!(lines = output.lines().count(), "Table rendered successfully");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Table with All Features
- Title and caption
- Header and footer
- Multiple box styles
- Styled cells
- Vertical alignment in cells

### Scenario 3: Width Constraints
- Table wider than terminal (must collapse)
- Table narrower than terminal (must expand)
- Columns at minimum width
- Ratio-based column distribution

### Scenario 4: Wide Characters in Cells
- CJK characters
- Emoji
- Mixed ASCII/CJK

### Scenario 5: Multi-line Cells
- Cells with newlines
- Cells that wrap
- Vertical alignment with multi-line

### Scenario 6: Edge Cases
- Empty table
- Single cell
- Very narrow width (force minimum)
- Very wide content

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
1. **Setup**: Table structure (columns, rows, options)
2. **Width calculation**: Input widths, available width, result widths
3. **Row rendering**: Cell content, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Box style, characters used
5. **Final output**: Complete rendered string
6. **Timing**: Time for width calc, time for render

## Golden Files
Create golden files for each scenario to detect visual regressions.

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_table.rs
- tests/golden/table_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All table scenarios pass. Width algorithms verified. Logs show calculation details.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:29.700982578-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 37 Table E2E tests covering all scenarios: simple tables, full features, width constraints, wide characters, alignment, edge cases, and snapshot tests","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.880043097-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.007910488-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-xz9","title":"Add Unit Tests for Markup Parser","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/markup/mod.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Basic Parsing
- [ ] Plain text (no markup)
- [ ] Single tag: [bold]text[/]
- [ ] Self-closing style application
- [ ] Named close: [/bold]

### Nested Tags
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/][/]
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/italic][/bold]
- [ ] Deep nesting (3+ levels)
- [ ] Sibling tags

### Style Inheritance
- [ ] Inner tag inherits outer
- [ ] Inner color overrides outer
- [ ] Attributes combine

### Escaping
- [ ] \[ produces [
- [ ] \] produces ]
- [ ] \\ produces \
- [ ] Mixed escaped and tags

### Error Cases
- [ ] Unclosed tag at EOF
- [ ] Unmatched close tag
- [ ] Invalid style in tag
- [ ] Empty tag []
- [ ] Malformed tag [bold
- [ ] No panic on any input

### Complex Cases
- [ ] [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] [bold red on blue]text[/]
- [ ] Multiple tags same line
- [ ] Tags with whitespace

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/markup/mod.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for markup parser. No panics on any input.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Markup parser tests must log the parsing process:

1. **Input**: The markup string being parsed
2. **Tokens**: Each token as it's recognized
3. **Stack**: Tag stack state during parsing
4. **Output**: Final segments with styles

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_nested_markup() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let input = "[bold][red]hello[/red][/bold]";
    info!(input = %input, "Parsing markup");
    
    let segments = render(input);
    
    for (i, seg) in segments.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            index = i,
            text = %seg.text,
            style = ?seg.style,
            "Segment"
        );
    }
    
    assert_eq!(segments.len(), 1);
    let style = segments[0].style.as_ref().unwrap();
    assert!(style.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    
    info!("Nested markup PASSED");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:11.457599519-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:01:59.465607497-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:01:59.465607497-05:00","close_reason":"Added 30 unit tests to markup parser (16→46 total). Covers: deep nesting (3-4 levels), sibling tags, style combination, escaping edge cases, empty/malformed input, unicode/emoji, no-panic verification, link params, handler syntax, hex colors. All 46 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-xz9","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:25.769330717-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

Implementation includes: Json struct with new()/from_str(), JsonTheme with all 7 color fields (key, string, number, boolean, null, bracket, punctuation), configurable indent/sort_keys/highlight options, proper string escaping, JsonError for invalid input. 24 unit tests cover all functionality including edge cases (empty objects/arrays, nested structures, custom themes). Library compiles with --features json.","labels":["feature-gated","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-yqr","title":"Create E2E Test: Panel and Nested Renderables","description":"## Objective
E2E tests for Panel rendering and nested renderable combinations.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Panel
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_basic_panel() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let panel = Panel::new("Hello World")
        .title("Greeting")
        .border_style(BoxStyle::ROUNDED);
    
    let console = Console::new().width(40);
    let output = console.render_to_string(&panel);
    
    debug!(output = %output, "Rendered panel");
    
    // Verify rounded corners
    assert!(output.contains('╭'), "Missing top-left corner");
    assert!(output.contains('╮'), "Missing top-right corner");
    assert!(output.contains("Greeting"), "Missing title");
    assert!(output.contains("Hello World"), "Missing content");
    
    info!("Panel test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Panel with Styled Content
- Content with markup: Panel::new("[bold]Important[/]")
- Verify: Markup rendered inside panel

### Scenario 3: Nested Panels
- Panel containing Panel
- Verify: Proper nesting, no border collision

### Scenario 4: Table in Panel
- Panel containing Table
- Verify: Width constraints propagate correctly

### Scenario 5: All Box Styles
- ASCII, ROUNDED, SQUARE, DOUBLE, HEAVY, MINIMAL
- Verify: Correct characters for each

### Scenario 6: Title Alignment
- Left, Center, Right title alignment
- Verify: Title position correct

## Logging Requirements
- Panel dimensions calculated
- Content measurement
- Border character selection
- Nested content rendering trace

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_panel.rs
- tests/golden/panel_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All panel scenarios pass. Nested renderables work correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:02.652711143-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:39:45.448364848-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:39:45.448364848-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 38 Panel E2E tests covering all scenarios: basic panels, styled content, box styles, title alignment, width/padding, wide characters, table in panel, and edge cases","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-yqr","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.927059312-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-yqr","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.052551956-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-yz6","title":"EPIC: Performance Optimization","description":"## Objective
Optimize rich_rust for production performance, focusing on hot paths identified through profiling.

## Background
Terminal rendering can be performance-critical in CLI tools. Key optimizations include:
1. Caching frequently computed values
2. Minimizing allocations in hot paths
3. Efficient string building for ANSI output
4. Lazy evaluation where possible

## Scope

### Caching (High Priority)
- LRU cache for color parsing
- Cell width caching for repeated strings
- Style ANSI code caching

### Allocation Reduction
- Reuse buffers in rendering loop
- SmallVec for small collections
- String interning for common values

### Algorithm Optimization
- Table width calculation
- Text wrapping
- Segment merging

### Benchmarks
- Establish baseline benchmarks
- Measure improvement after each optimization
- Prevent regressions

## Success Criteria
- Measurable improvement in benchmark suite
- No performance regressions
- Acceptable memory footprint","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmarks show improvement. ## Background
Terminal rendering can be performance-critical in CLI tools. Key optimizations include:
1. Caching frequently computed values
2. Minimizing allocations in hot paths
3. Efficient string building for ANSI output

### Caching (High Priority)

- Cell width caching for repeated strings
- Style ANSI code caching

- Text wrapping
- Segment merging

- Measure improvement after each optimization
- Prevent regressions

 {"id":"rich_rust-zbw","title":"Create Regression Test Suite","description":"## Objective
Create dedicated regression test suite to catch behavioral changes.

## Background
Regressions are bugs that reintroduce previously fixed issues or change expected behavior. A regression suite specifically tests known past issues and edge cases.

## Implementation

### Regression Test Structure
```
tests/
  regression/
    mod.rs           # Regression test module
    issue_001.rs     # Named after issue/bug
    issue_002.rs
    ...
```

### Regression Test Format
Each regression test must document:
1. What bug it prevents
2. When it was introduced
3. What the fix was
4. How to reproduce

```rust
/// Regression test for issue #42
/// 
/// Bug: Table width calculation overflowed when sum of minimums exceeded usize::MAX / 2
/// Fixed: 2024-01-15 in commit abc123
/// 
/// This test ensures the fix remains in place.
#[test]
fn regression_issue_42_table_width_overflow() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    info!("Regression test: issue #42 - table width overflow");
    
    // Reproduce the conditions that caused the bug
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("A").min_width(usize::MAX / 4))
        .add_column(Column::new("B").min_width(usize::MAX / 4))
        .add_column(Column::new("C").min_width(usize::MAX / 4));
    
    // This should not panic or overflow
    let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(|| {
        table.calculate_widths(100);
    });
    
    assert!(result.is_ok(), "Table width calculation panicked");
    info!("Regression test PASSED");
}
```

### Categories
1. **Parsing regressions**: Color, style, markup parsing edge cases
2. **Layout regressions**: Table width, text wrap, alignment
3. **Rendering regressions**: ANSI output, Unicode handling
4. **Performance regressions**: Speed, memory usage

### Running Regression Tests
```bash
# Run all regression tests
cargo test regression --features all

# Run specific category
cargo test regression::parsing
```

## Files to Create
- tests/regression/mod.rs
- tests/regression/*.rs (one per regression)","acceptance_criteria":"Regression suite established. Each test documents the bug it prevents.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:46.701299753-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:51:36.50075742-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:51:36.50075742-05:00","close_reason":"Created tests/regression_tests.rs with 35 comprehensive regression tests covering parsing, layout, and rendering categories. File includes documentation, test logging, and phased test organization as specified.","labels":["regression","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zbw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.344645233-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zbw","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.149145789-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-zca","title":"Add Unit Tests for Text Module","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/text.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Text Construction
- [ ] Text::new()
- [ ] Text::plain(text)
- [ ] Text::styled(text, style)
- [ ] From<String> and From<&str>

### Text Manipulation
- [ ] append() adds plain text
- [ ] append_styled() adds styled text
- [ ] stylize() applies span
- [ ] Overlapping spans
- [ ] Adjacent spans

### Text Rendering
- [ ] render() produces segments
- [ ] Styles applied correctly
- [ ] Tab expansion
- [ ] Base style + spans

### Text Wrapping
- [ ] wrap() at word boundaries
- [ ] wrap() breaks long words
- [ ] wrap() respects overflow mode
- [ ] wrap() with wide characters
- [ ] no_wrap flag

### Text Division (CRITICAL)
- [ ] divide() basic split
- [ ] divide() with spans crossing boundaries
- [ ] divide() span starts at cut
- [ ] divide() span ends at cut
- [ ] divide() multiple spans
- [ ] divide() empty divisions

### Justification
- [ ] Left justify
- [ ] Right justify
- [ ] Center justify
- [ ] Full justify

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/text.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for text.rs. divide() thoroughly tested.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Text module tests are CRITICAL and must have extensive logging:

1. **Text construction**: Log plain text and initial spans
2. **Stylize operations**: Log start/end positions and style
3. **Wrap operations**: Log width, input length, output line count
4. **Divide operations**: Log offsets and resulting parts WITH spans

Example for divide() - the most critical test:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_text_divide_with_spans() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("test_divide_spans");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let mut text = Text::plain("hello world!");
    info!(plain = %text.plain, "Initial text");
    
    text.stylize(6, 11, Style::new().bold());
    debug!(spans = ?text.spans, "After stylize");
    
    let parts = text.divide(&[5, 10]);
    for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            index = i,
            plain = %part.plain,
            spans = ?part.spans,
            "Divided part"
        );
    }
    
    // Detailed assertions with logging
    assert_eq!(parts[0].plain, "hello", "Part 0 content");
    assert!(parts[0].spans.is_empty(), "Part 0 should have no spans");
    debug!(part = 0, "Verified: no spans");
    
    assert_eq!(parts[1].plain, " worl", "Part 1 content");
    assert!(!parts[1].spans.is_empty(), "Part 1 should have span");
    debug!(part = 1, span = ?parts[1].spans[0], "Verified: has span");
    
    info!("Text divide with spans PASSED");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:47.51982787-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:11:47.199758675-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:11:47.199758675-05:00","close_reason":"Added 55 unit tests to Text module (19→74 total). Coverage: construction (new, styled, From), overlapping/adjacent/nested spans, divide() with span boundary cases (CRITICAL), wrap at word boundaries, overflow modes, justification (left/right/center/full), slice edge cases, tab expansion, span helpers. All 74 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zca","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.783337024-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-zt5","title":"Add Property-Based Tests","description":"## Objective
Add property-based tests using proptest or quickcheck for invariant verification.

## Test Properties

### Color Properties
- [ ] parse(x).to_hex() roundtrip preserves value (for valid hex)
- [ ] downgrade never increases color resolution
- [ ] all standard colors have valid ANSI codes

### Style Properties
- [ ] combine is associative: (a+b)+c = a+(b+c)
- [ ] null is identity: null+s = s, s+null = s
- [ ] parse(render(s)) roundtrip (where applicable)

### Text Properties
- [ ] wrap(w).join() preserves content (modulo whitespace)
- [ ] divide(cuts).concat() = original
- [ ] cell_length(wrap(w)) <= w for all lines

### Segment Properties
- [ ] split_at_cell(n) left + right = original
- [ ] simplify preserves rendered content
- [ ] divide(cuts) total length = original length

### Measurement Properties
- [ ] minimum <= maximum always
- [ ] clamp(a,b).minimum >= a
- [ ] clamp(a,b).maximum <= b
- [ ] union is commutative and associative

## Dependencies
- proptest crate in dev-dependencies

## Files to Create/Modify
- Cargo.toml (add proptest)
- tests/property_tests.rs (new file)","acceptance_criteria":"Property tests pass with 1000+ cases each. Key invariants verified.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:13.262394851-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:13.262394851-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-zt5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:25.87113382-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

       - name: Check compilation
         run: cargo check --all-features
+
+  # Security audit for known vulnerabilities
+  security:
+    name: Security Audit
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 10
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@56f84321dbccf38fb67ce29ab63e4754056677e0 # nightly
+
+      - name: Install cargo-audit
+        uses: taiki-e/install-action@735e5933943122c5ac182670a935f54a949265c1 # v2.52.4
+        with:
+          tool: cargo-audit
+
+      - name: Run security audit
+        run: cargo audit
+
+  # Documentation build check
+  docs:
+    name: Documentation
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 15
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@56f84321dbccf38fb67ce29ab63e4754056677e0 # nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry and build
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@9d47c6ad4b02e050fd481d890b2ea34778fd09d6 # v2.7.8
+        with:
+          key: docs
+
+      - name: Build documentation
+        run: cargo doc --all-features --no-deps
+        env:
+          RUSTDOCFLAGS: -D warnings
+
+      - name: Run doc tests
+        run: cargo test --doc --all-features
+
+  # Benchmark suite with artifact preservation
+  benchmarks:
+    name: Benchmarks
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 30
+    # Only run on push to master/main (benchmarks are noisy on PRs)
+    if: github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/master')
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@56f84321dbccf38fb67ce29ab63e4754056677e0 # nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry and build
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@9d47c6ad4b02e050fd481d890b2ea34778fd09d6 # v2.7.8
+        with:
+          key: benchmarks
+
+      - name: Run benchmarks
+        run: cargo bench --all-features -- --noplot 2>&1 | tee bench-output.log
+
+      - name: Upload benchmark results
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@b4b15b8c7c6ac21ea08fcf65892d2ee8f75cf882 # v4.4.3
+        with:
+          name: benchmark-results
+          path: |
+            bench-output.log
+            target/criterion
+          retention-days: 90
+
+  # Code coverage with codecov integration
+  coverage:
+    name: Coverage
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 30
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@56f84321dbccf38fb67ce29ab63e4754056677e0 # nightly
+        with:
+          components: llvm-tools-preview
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry and build
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@9d47c6ad4b02e050fd481d890b2ea34778fd09d6 # v2.7.8
+        with:
+          key: coverage
+
+      - name: Install cargo-llvm-cov
+        uses: taiki-e/install-action@735e5933943122c5ac182670a935f54a949265c1 # v2.52.4
+        with:
+          tool: cargo-llvm-cov
+
+      - name: Generate coverage report
+        run: |
+          cargo llvm-cov --all-features --workspace --lcov --output-path lcov.info
+          cargo llvm-cov report --all-features --workspace --html --output-dir coverage-html
+
+      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
+        uses: codecov/codecov-action@1e68e06f1dbfde0e4cefc87efeba9e4643565f39 # v5.1.2
+        with:
+          files: lcov.info
+          fail_ci_if_error: false
+          verbose: true
+
+      - name: Upload coverage HTML report
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@b4b15b8c7c6ac21ea08fcf65892d2ee8f75cf882 # v4.4.3
+        with:
+          name: coverage-report
+          path: coverage-html
+          retention-days: 30
